tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

params.text: LA Los Angeles trip hotels preferences

content.text:
No existing memories about LA trips. The user wants hotel recommendations for LA and wants me to remember them. I should provide some well-known, highly-regarded hotel recommendations for Los Angeles and save them as a memory.

I'll give a range of options across different price points and neighborhoods.

No previous notes on LA trips! Here are some great hotel recommendations across different areas and budgets in Los Angeles:

### 🌟 Luxury
1. **The Peninsula Beverly Hills** – Iconic 5-star hotel with exceptional service, rooftop pool, and a prime Beverly Hills location.
2. **Shutters on the Beach (Santa Monica)** – Right on the sand, stunning ocean views, a beach-luxury vibe.
3. **The West Hollywood EDITION** – Trendy, modern, great rooftop pool and nightlife scene.

### 💎 Upscale / Boutique
4. **The LINE LA (Koreatown)** – Stylish boutique hotel with great dining (Openaire restaurant), centrally located.
5. **Hotel Figueroa (Downtown LA)** – Beautiful Spanish Colonial design, rooftop pool, walkable to DTLA attractions.
6. **Santa Monica Proper Hotel** – Chic design hotel steps from the beach and Third Street Promenade.

### 💰 Great Value
7. **Freehand Los Angeles (Downtown LA)** – Trendy, affordable, rooftop pool, great social atmosphere.
8. **The Ambrose Hotel (Santa Monica)** – Quiet, eco-friendly boutique hotel at a reasonable price near the beach.

Let me save these for you now!

params.title: LA Hotel Recommendations for Upcoming Trip
params.content:
Planning a trip to Los Angeles next week. Hotel recommendations:

LUXURY:
- The Peninsula Beverly Hills – 5-star, rooftop pool, Beverly Hills
- Shutters on the Beach – Santa Monica, beachfront
- The West Hollywood EDITION – trendy, great rooftop and nightlife

UPSCALE/BOUTIQUE:
- The LINE LA – Koreatown, stylish, great dining
- Hotel Figueroa – Downtown LA, Spanish Colonial, rooftop pool
- Santa Monica Proper Hotel – chic, near beach and Third Street Promenade

GREAT VALUE:
- Freehand Los Angeles – Downtown LA, affordable, rooftop pool
- The Ambrose Hotel – Santa Monica, eco-friendly, budget-friendly

result.content:
Planning a trip to Los Angeles next week. Hotel recommendations:

LUXURY:
- The Peninsula Beverly Hills – 5-star, rooftop pool, Beverly Hills
- Shutters on the Beach – Santa Monica, beachfront
- The West Hollywood EDITION – trendy, great rooftop and nightlife

UPSCALE/BOUTIQUE:
- The LINE LA – Koreatown, stylish, great dining
- Hotel Figueroa – Downtown LA, Spanish Colonial, rooftop pool
- Santa Monica Proper Hotel – chic, near beach and Third Street Promenade

GREAT VALUE:
- Freehand Los Angeles – Downtown LA, affordable, rooftop pool
- The Ambrose Hotel – Santa Monica, eco-friendly, budget-friendly
result.title: LA Hotel Recommendations for Upcoming Trip

response.delta: 've saved all the recommendations! ✅ Here's a quick summary:

- **For splurging** → The Peninsula Beverly Hills or Shutters on the Beach
- **For a

cool vibe** → The LINE LA or Hotel Figueroa
- **For saving money** → Freehand LA or The Ambrose Hotel

Would you like more
details on any of these, or do you have preferences (location, budget, etc.)
that could help narrow it down? I can also look into restaurants
or things to do in LA! 🌴

tools.name: set-alarm
tools.description:
Schedule an alarm to wake yourself up in the future.
Provide exactly one of "in" (a duration from now) or "at" (an absolute time).
When the alarm fires you will receive a prompt and can continue working.
Setting a new alarm replaces any previously scheduled one.

in.description: Duration from now expressed as "<number> <unit>", e.g. "30 seconds", "5 minutes", "2 hours".

at.description: Absolute ISO-8601 timestamp to wake at, e.g. "2026-06-04T18:00:00.000Z".

tools.name: get-current-date
tools.description: Get the current date and time as an ISO-8601 string.

timezone.description: Optional IANA timezone name. Defaults to the process clock when omitted.

  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.

tools.name: set-alarm
tools.description:
Schedule an alarm to wake yourself up in the future.
Provide exactly one of "in" (a duration from now) or "at" (an absolute time).
When the alarm fires you will receive a prompt and can continue working.
Setting a new alarm replaces any previously scheduled one.

in.description: Duration from now expressed as "<number> <unit>", e.g. "30 seconds", "5 minutes", "2 hours".

at.description: Absolute ISO-8601 timestamp to wake at, e.g. "2026-06-04T18:00:00.000Z".

tools.name: get-current-date
tools.description: Get the current date and time as an ISO-8601 string.

timezone.description: Optional IANA timezone name. Defaults to the process clock when omitted.

tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
